goal: Prove that Cortex can ingest artifacts, consolidate them into durable knowledge, retrieve them later, and improve a chat answer without requiring the user to restate source context.
user_goals: Ingest a document once and later ask ordinary questions without restating its contents.
Add a quick capture and have Cortex reinforce or extend the existing knowledge base.
Bring up required service dependencies and confirm they are healthy and usable.
Inspect provenance, retrieval traces, and service posture when behavior is wrong.
See a visible before-and-after proof that Cortex improved a chat answer.
preconditions: OpenSearch and Git Service foundations are implemented enough for the MVP path to run end to end.
Artifact storage and provider routing are configured for the demo corpus.
A small demo corpus is available for import.
At least one quick capture scenario is prepared.
A minimal chat proof surface exists for the final acceptance pass.

tests.title: Infrastructure And Identity Foundation
tests.objective: Prove the MVP has working dependency substrate and stable artifact identity before end-to-end knowledge tests begin.
preconditions: The OpenSearch epic and Git Service epic are implemented.
Artifact storage and the Git provider are available to the project.
procedure: Validate the OpenSearch service through its epic-level test plan.
Validate the Git substrate and provider path for stored artifacts.
Create or register one imported artifact and one capture artifact.
Resolve both artifacts by Cortex-owned artifact ID.
expected_results: Required services are healthy and reachable.
Imported and captured artifacts resolve through Cortex-owned identity.
Artifact resolution does not depend on source-system path stability.
failure_conditions: Required services are unavailable or unhealthy.
Artifact identity depends on source path or provider-specific path conventions.
Stored artifacts cannot be reopened by artifact ID.

tests.title: Baseline Knowledge Seeding
tests.objective: Prove imported artifacts become durable knowledge objects with provenance and retrievable state.
preconditions: Demo corpus is selected and available.
Import and extraction paths are wired to artifact storage and OpenSearch.
procedure: Import a small project document set.
Confirm Cortex-managed imported copies exist in artifact storage.
Confirm knowledge objects or knowledge deltas were created.
Run retrieval queries against the imported corpus.
expected_results: Imported documents become source-linked knowledge state.
Knowledge is retrievable with source-linked provenance.
Imported artifacts remain reachable by Cortex artifact ID even if source paths drift.
failure_conditions: Import succeeds but no durable knowledge state is created.
Retrieved results do not point back to imported evidence.
Artifact resolution breaks when the source system path changes.

tests.title: Quick Capture Refines Existing Knowledge
tests.objective: Prove quick capture uses the same durable memory path as imported artifacts and can reinforce existing knowledge.
preconditions: Imported project knowledge already exists from AT-002.
procedure: Submit a short capture related to the imported corpus.
Confirm the capture is stored as a capture artifact.
Inspect the resulting knowledge delta or object change.
Re-run retrieval on the affected topic.
expected_results: The capture artifact is persisted and addressable by artifact ID.
The capture reinforces or extends existing knowledge rather than duplicating blindly.
The updated topic becomes retrievable through the same knowledge path.
failure_conditions: Quick capture bypasses the normal knowledge-object model.
Capture artifacts cannot be reopened or traced.
Obvious duplicate objects are created instead of reinforcement or extension.

tests.title: Multi-Angle Retrieval And Provenance
tests.objective: Prove the same underlying concept can be found from multiple framings and audited back to its source evidence.
preconditions: Imported knowledge and at least one related capture exist.
procedure: Run retrieval with multiple valid phrasings that should resolve to the same concept.
Choose one returned object from the results.
Inspect its retrieval trace and provenance.
Open the linked imported or capture artifact.
expected_results: The same underlying concept is discoverable from multiple phrasings.
Ranking may vary, but the canonical object remains reachable.
The retrieved object can be traced back to the evidence that created or reinforced it.
failure_conditions: Different phrasings split the same concept into disconnected result sets.
Retrieval traces do not explain filters and ranking contributors.
Provenance cannot be followed back to an artifact.

tests.title: Chat Injection Proof Surface
tests.objective: Prove Cortex retrieval materially improves at least one ordinary chat answer and remains inspectable.
preconditions: Imported artifacts and at least one related quick capture already exist.
A minimal chat harness or proof surface exists.
procedure: Ask an ordinary chat question that should benefit from stored Cortex knowledge.
Inspect the retrieved bundle or trace used for the answer.
Compare the answer with a no-Cortex or retrieval-disabled baseline.
expected_results: Cortex retrieval runs before final answer generation.
The retrieved bundle is bounded and inspectable.
The final answer is materially better than the no-Cortex baseline.
failure_conditions: Chat never calls Cortex retrieval before answering.
The retrieved bundle cannot be inspected.
The answer quality does not improve meaningfully when Cortex retrieval is enabled.

exit_criteria: All project acceptance tests pass or have an explicitly approved exception.
Required evidence exists for each project acceptance test.
At least one visible chat proof demonstrates a materially improved answer due to Cortex retrieval.
No unresolved failure remains that breaks artifact identity, provenance, retrieval usefulness, or chat-time value.
